title: scrophularia auriculata subsp. lyrata
intro: Scrophularia auriculata subsp. lyrata, also known as the Lyrata-flowered Scrophularia or Dragon's Blood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Scrophularia auriculata subsp. lyrata can be prepared in various forms, including tea, tincture, and infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
